title: AuditEntry
description: One line of `skills.audit.jsonl` per spec §8.security L5 (M05 Stage E). The audit log is append-only, newline-delimited JSON written by the runtime's safety primitives (framework_loader, capability_enforcer, tier_evaluator) on every grant / denial / transition / gap event. v0.1 ships unbounded growth + no hash-chain + no per-event provenance block — those land post-v0.1. Phase doc M05-gap-capability.md §E is the merged contract. The `details` field is open-shape per-`kind`; consumers read `kind` and interpret `details` accordingly. Each entry carries `timestamp_unix_ms` + `session_id` so a streaming reader can correlate entries to a session even though v0.1 is single-session per §0d.

timestamp_unix_ms.description: Unix millisecond timestamp when the entry was written. UTC; captured from system clock at write time.

details.description: Open-shape payload per `kind`. See `runtime_main::audit::entry` for the per-kind constructors that pin the per-kind shape at the call site; this schema intentionally leaves it open so v0.1 can extend `details` per kind without a schema major bump.

AuditEntryKind.title: AuditEntryKind
AuditEntryKind.description: Discriminator for the `details` shape. v0.1 emits six kinds: `framework_loaded` (Stage A successful load), `gap_detected` (Stage A walker emitted a gap), `gap_resolved` (Stage A resolution event), `capability_granted` (Stage B enforcer accepted), `capability_denied` (Stage B enforcer rejected), `tier_transition` (Stage D evaluator-state flip). Per gotcha #43 (typify-friendly extraction): enum extracted to $defs with title.

AuditSessionId.title: AuditSessionId
AuditSessionId.description: Session identifier carried per-entry so a v1.0+ multi-session streaming reader can correlate audit lines to a session. v0.1 single-session per §0d uses a synthetic id at session start. Per gotcha #43 (typify-friendly extraction): validated inline string extracted to $defs with title + minLength.
